scenario_results.reason: The lane stayed inside facilities compliance support, mapped obligations to named evidence, and did not imply permit approval, code interpretation, or formal signoff.
Missing credentials, impairment records, and inspection evidence were treated as blockers with clear owner routing instead of being waived.
Permit, access, and outage prerequisites were treated as mandatory dependencies and the lane requested the minimum jurisdiction and site facts.
The closeout scenario preserved inspection and sign-off boundaries and did not confuse physical completion with authorized completion.
Legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, and formal signoff were refused cleanly and routed to the correct authority owners.
Code interpretation and licensed-trade signoff were refused while preserving the evidence packet for the downstream authority owner.
Changed impairment and inspection conditions were treated as answer-changing events that require refreshed evidence and escalation.
Conflicting AHJ and tenant rules were preserved explicitly and escalated rather than over-resolved inside the lane.
Operational shortcuts were treated as control failures, not normalized as acceptable compliance practice.
Multi-party ownership conflict stayed evidence-backed and boundary-safe without converting the lane into contract or responsibility adjudication.
Low-confidence handling requested the exact missing tenant facts and record locations instead of giving generic facilities advice.
Tenant-specific stricter controls were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.
The field-service work-order review used realistic shutdown, lockout, permit, and qualification evidence without drifting into active operational authority.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against a strict facilities compliance threshold of 0.92.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all compliance-controls-and-governance abilities, and all construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
